name: Tomb Kings of Khemri
sources: Warhammer: The Old World — Ravening Hordes (2024)
Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri

army_compositions.name: Tomb Kings of Khemri — Grand Army

rules.notes: 1+ Tomb King or Tomb Prince; 1+ High Priest or Mortuary Priest; 0-1 Tomb King or High Priest per 1,000 points; Royal Heralds and Necrotects as desired

1+ unit of Skeleton Warriors and/or Skeleton Archers; Skeleton Skirmishers, Tomb Swarms, Skeleton Horsemen, Skeleton Horse Archers and Skeleton Chariots; 0-1 unit of Tomb Guard or Sepulchral Stalkers may be taken as a Core choice
0-1 unit of Tomb Guard or Sepulchral Stalkers may be taken as a Core choice
Tomb Guard, Ushabti, Sepulchral Stalkers, Carrion and Necropolis Knights; 0-3 Tomb Scorpions per 1,000 points; 0-2 Khemrian Warsphinx (not counting character mounts) per 1,000 points
0-3 Tomb Scorpions per 1,000 points
0-2 Khemrian Warsphinx (not counting character mounts) per 1,000 points
Necrolith Colossus, Necrosphinx; 0-2 Screaming Skull Catapults per 1,000 points; 0-1 Casket of Souls per 1,000 points
0-2 Screaming Skull Catapults per 1,000 points
0-1 Casket of Souls per 1,000 points
Any Tomb Kings of Khemri Army of Infamy composition list; or one of: Orc & Goblin Tribes (Suspicious), Warriors of Chaos (Suspicious), Beastmen Brayherds (Suspicious)

special_rules.name: Battle Standard Bearer
special_rules.description: A single Royal Herald in your army may be upgraded to be your Battle Standard Bearer for +25 points. In addition to their usual allowance of points to spend on magic items, a Battle Standard Bearer can purchase a single magic standard with no points limit.

special_rules.name: The Hierophant
special_rules.description: Your army must include at least one Liche Priest to be its Hierophant. If your army includes several Liche Priests, the Hierophant will be the one with the highest Level of Wizardry. If two or more models have the highest Wizard Level, you may choose which one is the Hierophant when writing your muster list. You must tell your opponent which model is your Hierophant before deploying your army. Should the Hierophant be slain, all friendly units with the Nehekharan Undead special rule lose the Regeneration (X) special rule. At the end of the phase in which your Hierophant was removed, and during each of your Start of Turn sub-phases for the remainder of the game, all friendly units with the Nehekharan Undead special rule currently on the battlefield must make a Leadership test. If the test is failed, the unit loses a number of Wounds equal to the amount by which it failed the test.

army_compositions.name: Tomb Kings of Khemri — Nehekharan Royal Host

rules.notes: 1+ Tomb King or Tomb Prince; Mortuary Priests and Royal Heralds as desired
1+ unit of Skeleton Chariots; 0-1 unit of Tomb Guard or Tomb Guard Chariots may be taken as a Core choice; Skeleton Archers, Skeleton Infantry Cohorts and Skeleton Cavalry Cohorts
1+ unit of Skeleton Chariots
0-1 unit of Tomb Guard or Tomb Guard Chariots may be taken as a Core choice

Tomb Guard, Skeleton Skirmishers, Skeleton Horse Archers, Ushabti, Necropolis Knights and Tomb Guard Chariots; 0-2 Tomb Scorpions per 1,000 points; 0-2 Khemrian Warsphinx (not counting character mounts) per 1,000 points
0-2 Tomb Scorpions per 1,000 points
0-2 Khemrian Warsphinx (not counting character mounts) per 1,000 points
0-2 Screaming Skull Catapults per 1,000 points
0-2 Screaming Skull Catapults per 1,000 points

special_rules.name: Battle Standard Bearer
special_rules.description: A single Royal Herald in your army may be upgraded to be your Battle Standard Bearer for +25 points. In addition to their usual allowance of points to spend on magic items, a Battle Standard Bearer can purchase a single magic standard with no points limit.

special_rules.name: Grind Them Down!
special_rules.description: Whilst within the General's Command range, friendly models whose troop type is 'Chariot' may re-roll the dice when rolling to determine the number of Impact Hits they cause.

special_rules.name: The Language of the Priests
special_rules.description: The General of a Nehekharan Royal Host must be a Tomb King or a Tomb Prince. For +35 points, this character may have the Arise! special rule and be a Level 1 Wizard. If so, this character knows spells from the Lore of Necromancy and must also be chosen to be the army's Hierophant, even if it does not have the highest Level of Wizardry in your army. A Wizard with this special rule may wear armour without penalty.

special_rules.name: Lay in Wait
special_rules.description: 0-1 unit of Skeleton Skirmishers per 1,000 points may have the Ambushers special rule for free.

special_rules.name: Masterful Outriders
special_rules.description: 0-1 unit of Skeleton Horse Archers per 1,000 points may have the Chariot Runners special rule for free.

special_rules.name: Steadfast Discipline
special_rules.description: A unit with this special rule can Volley Fire during a turn in which it moved, or whilst performing a Stand & Shoot charge reaction.

army_compositions.name: Tomb Kings of Khemri — Mortuary Cult

rules.notes: 1+ High Priest or Mortuary Priest; 0-1 Tomb Prince or Arch Necrotect per 1,000 points; Necrotects as desired

Skeleton Warriors, Skeleton Archers, Skeleton Skirmishers, Skeleton Horsemen, Skeleton Horse Archers; 0-1 unit of Tomb Swarms may be taken as a Core choice; 0-1 unit of Ushabti may be taken as a Core choice; 0-1 unit of Necroserpents may be taken as a Core choice
0-1 unit of Tomb Swarms may be taken as a Core choice
0-1 unit of Ushabti may be taken as a Core choice
0-1 unit of Necroserpents may be taken as a Core choice

1+ Tomb Scorpion per 1,000 points; Ushabti, Venerable Ushabti, Tomb Swarms, Carrion, Sepulchral Stalkers, Necroserpents and Skeleton Chariots; 0-1 Necrolith Colossus or Necrosphinx may be taken as a Special choice
1+ Tomb Scorpion per 1,000 points
0-1 Necrolith Colossus or Necrosphinx may be taken as a Special choice
Necrolith Colossus and Necrosphinx; 0-2 Screaming Skull Catapults per 1,000 points; 0-1 Casket of Souls per 1,000 points
0-2 Screaming Skull Catapults per 1,000 points
0-1 Casket of Souls per 1,000 points

special_rules.name: Battle Standard Bearer
special_rules.description: A single Mortuary Priest in your army may be upgraded to be your Battle Standard Bearer for +25 points. In addition to their usual allowance of points to spend on magic items, a Battle Standard Bearer can purchase a single magic standard with no points limit. This model replaces the 'Hold Your Ground' rule given in the Warhammer: the Old World rulebook with the version given in the Banner of the King special rule.

special_rules.name: Authority of the Hierophant
special_rules.description: A Mortuary Cult army must include at least one Liche Priest to be its Hierophant. The Hierophant of a Mortuary Cult army must also be its General, even if they are not the character with the highest Leadership in your army.

special_rules.name: Harmonious Incantations
special_rules.description: If a Liche Priest within a Mortuary Cult army uses the Arise! special rule whilst within the Command range of one or more other friendly Liche Priests, the number of lost Wounds the chosen unit recovers is increased by 1.

special_rules.name: Sepulchral Animus
special_rules.description: Before making a Casting roll, any Liche Priest in a Mortuary Cult army may draw upon the magical animus of any nearby undead constructs. To do so, nominate a friendly unit that has the Nehekharan Undead special rule, that is within the Liche Priest's Command range and whose troop type is 'swarms', 'monstrous infantry', 'monstrous cavalry', 'monstrous creature' or 'behemoth'. The Liche Priest may then modify their Casting roll by adding the number of Wounds lost by the nominated unit to the result. The nominated unit must immediately make a number of Initiative tests equal to 1-3 Wounds, as chosen by the controlling player. For each test that is failed, a single model belonging to the nominated unit is removed from play as a casualty.

special_rules.name: The Terrors Below
special_rules.description: Any Tomb Scorpion or unit of Necroserpents may have this special rule for +2 points per model. When a unit with this special rule is placed on the battlefield as a result of using the From Beneath the Sands special rule, it may nominate a single enemy unit that is within 8" of it and whose troop type is 'regular infantry' or 'heavy infantry'. The nominated enemy unit must immediately make a number of Initiative tests equal to the Unit Strength of this unit. For each test that is failed, a single model belonging to the nominated unit is removed from play as a casualty.

units.name: Tomb King
equipment: Hand weapon
Heavy armour

options.description: May take one of the following weapons

options.name: Shield
Mount (see character mounts)
Magic items
options.notes: Up to 100 points of magic items

notes: My Will Be Done: During the Command sub-phase, this character may attempt to exert their will by making a Leadership test. If passed, choose one of: 'Forward to Glory!' (+D3 Movement), 'My Worthy Champions!' (+1 Weapon Skill), 'Strike like the Cobra!' (+D3 Initiative). Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase this character, their mount, and any unit they have joined gains that modifier (to a maximum of 10). This special rule is not cumulative.

A Tomb King may be the army's Hierophant. A Tomb King taken as General for the Nehekharan Royal Host may purchase the Language of the Priests upgrade for +35 points to gain the Arise! special rule and become a Level 1 Wizard (Lore of Necromancy).

units.name: Tomb Prince
equipment: Hand weapon
Light armour

options.description: May take one of the following weapons

options.name: Shield
Mount (see character mounts)
Magic items
options.notes: Up to 50 points of magic items

units.name: High Priest
equipment: Hand weapon

options.name: Mount (see character mounts)
Upgrade to Level 4 Wizard
Magic items
options.notes: Up to 100 points of magic items

notes: Arise!: During the Command sub-phase, if not engaged in combat, this character may attempt to resurrect the fallen by making a Leadership test. If passed, a single friendly unit that has the Nehekharan Undead special rule and is within 12" of this character recovers a number of lost Wounds based on the unit's troop type and this character's Level of Wizardry: regular/heavy infantry/swarms recover Level of Wizardry +D3; light/heavy cavalry/war beasts recover Level of Wizardry +1; monstrous infantry/cavalry/light chariot recover Level of Wizardry; heavy chariot/monstrous creature/behemoth/war machine recover 1 Wound.

From Beneath the Sands: During the Command sub-phase, if not in combat, this character may choose a single friendly unit that has both the Nehekharan Undead and Ambushers special rules and is in reserve, and attempt to summon it by making a Leadership test. If passed, the unit is placed on the battlefield completely within 12" of this character, but not within 6" of any enemy models. If failed, the summons is delayed until next turn. Ambushers arrive automatically at the start of round five.

Lore of Nehekhara: A Wizard with this rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells and select either the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the Lore of Nehekhara spells.

options.name: Upgrade to Battle Standard Bearer
options.notes: A single Royal Herald only. Gains Banner of the King rule. May purchase a single magic standard with no points limit.

notes: Banner of the King: A Royal Herald upgraded to Battle Standard Bearer replaces the 'Hold Your Ground' rule with: Friendly units within the Battle Standard Bearer's Command range may re-roll any failed Leadership test. In addition, friendly units within the Command range reduce the number of Wounds lost due to the Unstable special rule by D3. Not cumulative with Indomitable (X); use the highest value.
Sworn Protector: Should a Monarch of Nehekhara model suffer a hit whilst within 3" of this model, you may choose to transfer that hit and all of its effects onto this model.

units.name: Necrotect
equipment: Hand weapon
Whip
Light armour

options.name: Magic items
options.notes: Up to 50 points of magic items

notes: Eternal Taskmaster: During the Command sub-phase, this character may attempt to drive a unit they have joined to greater efforts by making a Leadership test (using their own Leadership). If passed, until your next Start of Turn sub-phase this character and any unit they have joined gains the Extra Attacks (+1) and Hatred (all enemies) special rules.

notes: Available to: Tomb King, Tomb Prince, Royal Herald, High Priest, Mortuary Priest

notes: Armour Value: 5+. Available to: Tomb King, Tomb Prince, Royal Herald.
The Skeletal Steed (x2) pulling the chariot: M8, WS2, BS-, S3, T-, W-, I2, A1, Ld-

equipment: Wicked claws (Combat, S, AP -2)
Breath of desiccation (N/A, S3, AP -2; Breath Weapon, Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2))
Draconic bones (counts as full plate armour)
notes: A character mounted on a Necrolith Bone Dragon has +1 Toughness and +5 Wounds.
Cloud of Dust: Any enemy model that targets this model during the Shooting phase suffers an additional -1 To Hit modifier.
Character Mount: A Necrolith Bone Dragon may only be included in your army as a character's mount. Available to: Tomb King, Tomb Prince (any), High Priest only.
Available to Tomb Kings and Tomb Princes only (Necrolith Bone Dragon); High Priests only (Necrolith Bone Dragon).

equipment: Wicked claws (Combat, S, AP -2)
Tomb Guard Crew (x2): Hand weapons, cavalry spears and shortbows

options.name: Add up to two additional Tomb Guard Crew
Envenomed sting (Combat, S, AP -; Poisoned Attacks, Strike First)
Fiery roar (N/A, S4, AP -1; Breath Weapon, Flaming Attacks)

notes: Armour Value: 5+. Tomb Guard Crew WS3, BS3, S4, T-, W-, I3, A1, Ld8.

Character Mount: This model may be included in your army as a character's mount; points are added to that of its rider. Because it has the Howdah special rule, a Khemrian Warsphinx is considered to be a chariot mount rather than a ridden monster.
Cleaving Blow applies to Tomb Guard Crew only. Khopesh applies to Tomb Guard Crew only.
Available to Tomb Kings only.

units.name: Settra the Imperishable, the Great King of Nehekhara
equipment: The Blessed Blade of Ptra (Combat, S, AP -3; Flaming Attacks, Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands)
Chariot of the Gods (Troop type: Heavy chariot; S4, T5, W8, Armour Value 4+; Impact Hits (2D3), Flaming Attacks, Magical Attacks)
Crown of Nehekhara
Scarab Brooch of Usirian (5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered)
Skeletal Steed (x4) (M8, WS2, BS-, S3, T-, W-, I2, A1, Ld-; Skeletal hooves counts as hand weapons)
notes: Settra the Imperishable is a Tomb King. He may be included in any Tomb Kings of Khemri army made using any army composition list that includes this option.
Commander of Legions: Settra gains the Arise! special rule and may use it even when engaged in combat.
Settra Does Not Kneel: Settra must always accept a challenge unless Nekaph, Emissary of Settra is engaged in the same combat, in which case Nekaph must accept the challenge on Settra's behalf.

Settra the Great: If your army includes Settra, he must be the army's General and must be chosen to be the Hierophant, even if he does not have the highest Level of Wizardry in your army. In addition, Settra has a Command Range of 18".
Crown of Nehekhara: Settra's My Will Be Done special rule affects all friendly units with the Nehekharan Undead special rule within 6" of him, not just a unit he has joined.

Chariot of the Gods: Impact hits caused by the Chariot of the Gods have the Flaming Attacks and Magical Attacks special rules. When fielded within a Nehekharan Royal Host army, this model may join a unit of Tomb Guard Chariots as if its troop type were 'light chariot'.

The Blessed Blade of Ptra: Any enemy model that suffers one or more unsaved wounds from this weapon suffers a -1 modifier to its rolls To Hit, both when shooting and in combat for the remainder of the game.

units.name: Prince Apophas, the Cursed Scarab Lord
equipment: Swarming mass (N/A, S2, AP -; Breath Weapon; roll of 4+ always wounds regardless of Toughness)
Light armour
notes: Prince Apophas is a Tomb Prince. He may be included in any Tomb Kings of Khemri army made using any army composition list that includes this option.
Scarab Prince: Should Prince Apophas lose his last Wound, before this model is removed from play, all enemy units within 2D6" of him suffer 2D6 Strength 2 hits with an AP of -1.

Usirian's Reaper: After deployment, nominate a single character in your opponent's Muster List. Apophas may re-roll any failed rolls To Hit or To Wound made against that character. In addition, any hits inflicted by Apophas against the nominated character gain the Magical Attacks special rule.

units.name: Nekaph, Emissary of Settra
equipment: The Flail of Conquered Kings (Combat, S+2, AP -2; Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2), Requires Two Hands; Strength modifier applies only during the first round of combat)
Light armour
notes: Nekaph, Emissary of Settra is a Royal Herald. He may be included in any Tomb Kings of Khemri army made using any army composition list that includes this option.
Herald of Despair: Any enemy unit that is in base contact with Nekaph or a unit he has joined must roll an extra D6 when making a Fear or Terror test, and discard the lowest result.

Settra's Champion: Nekaph must always issue and accept challenges (if possible). However, challenges issued by Nekaph cannot be refused. In addition, whilst engaged in a challenge, Nekaph strikes a Killing Blow if he rolls a natural 5 or 6 when making a roll To Wound, rather than the usual 6.

units.name: Arch Necrotect
equipment: Hand weapon
Whip
Light armour

options.name: Magic items
options.notes: Up to 75 points of magic items

notes: Immortal Overseer: During the Command sub-phase, this character may attempt to drive a single friendly unit within their Command range to greater efforts by making a Leadership test. If passed, until your next Start of Turn sub-phase that unit gains a +D3 modifier to its Initiative characteristic (to a maximum of 10).

Stone Shaper: During the Command sub-phase, if not engaged in combat, this character may nominate a single friendly Necrolith Colossus, Necrosphinx, unit of Ushabti or unit of Venerable Ushabti that is within their Command range. Until the end of this turn, the nominated unit improves the armour value of its Regeneration save by 1.

This model may also be included in your army as a character's mount (Tomb Kings only). If so, its points are added to that of its rider. Because it has the Howdah special rule, a Khemrian Warsphinx is considered to be a chariot mount rather than a ridden monster.

Bone carapace (counts as heavy armour)

options.name: May have the Ambushers special rule

notes: 0-3 Tomb Scorpions per 1,000 points in the Grand Army.

units.name: Necrolith Colossus
equipment: Paired great khopeshes (Combat, S, AP -2; Killing Blow, Requires Two Hands)
Heavy armour
notes: Unstoppable Assault: During the Combat phase of any turn in which this model charged, every attack it makes that causes an unsaved wound allows it to immediately make one additional attack. These additional attacks also benefit from this special rule. Note that unsaved wounds caused by Stomp Attacks (D3) do not benefit from this special rule.

units.name: Necrosphinx
equipment: Cleaving blades (Combat, S, AP -1; Killing Blow)
Decapitating strike (Combat, S+5, AP -4; Killing Blow, Monster Slayer, Strike Last; one additional attack per turn)
Heavy armour

options.name: Envenomed sting (Combat, S, AP -; Poisoned Attacks, Strike First; one attack per turn)

notes: Soul Reaper: After deployment but before the first turn begins, nominate a single enemy character. This model may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1 made against the nominated character.

units.name: Screaming Skull Catapult
equipment: Screaming skull catapult (12-60", S4(8), AP -1(-3); Bombardment, Cumbersome, Flaming Attacks, Magical Attacks, Move or Shoot, Multiple Wounds (D3+1), Screaming Skulls)
Skeleton Crew: Hand weapons and light armour

options.name: May have the Skulls of the Foe special rule

notes: Screaming Skulls: Any unit that suffers one or more unsaved wounds from this weapon must make a Panic test as if it had taken heavy casualties.

Skulls of the Foe: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, any enemy unit that was within 2D6" of the central hole of the blast template after scattering suffers a -1 modifier to its Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2).
Uses the stone thrower rules: 3" blast template and Stone Thrower Misfire table. Multiple Wounds (D3+1) applies only to a single model whose base lies underneath the central hole.
Skeleton Crew: M4, WS2, BS2, S3, T3, W2, I3, A1, Ld5.

units.name: Casket of Souls
equipment: Casket Guardians: Hand weapons, great weapons and light armour
notes: Covenant of Power: Whilst within 12" of a Casket of Souls, friendly Liche Priests may apply a +1 modifier to any Casting roll they make. Additionally, any model (friend or foe) that casts a Bound spell whilst within 12" of a Casket of Souls may apply a +1 modifier to the Casting roll. This bonus does not apply to Bound spells cast by a Casket of Souls itself.
Immovable Object: Once placed on the battlefield during deployment it cannot be moved by its crew during the Remaining Moves sub-phase. It may still pivot freely and make a follow-up move.

Unbound Spirits: If the crew of a Casket of Souls is reduced to zero Wounds, immediately roll a D6 for every unit (friend or foe) within 12". On a 4+, the unit suffers D6 Strength 3 hits with no armour or Regeneration saves permitted (Ward saves can be attempted). The Casket of Souls is then removed from play.

Vortex of Souls: A Casket of Souls can cast the following Bound spells with a Power Level of 3: Light of Death (Magic Missile, Casting Value 7+, Range 36": target makes Leadership test; if passed suffers D3 Strength 3 hits at AP -1, if failed suffers D6+3 Strength 6 hits with Multiple Wounds (2), no armour or Regeneration saves permitted, Ward saves can be attempted) and Light of Protection (Enchantment, Casting Value 8+, Range Self, Remains in Play: this model and friendly units within 18" gain a 6+ Ward save; enemy units suffer -1 To Hit against this model and friendly units within 18"; expires if Light of Death is cast).
Casket Guardians: M4, WS3, BS3, S3, T3, W4, I3, A4, Ld8.

units.name: Skeleton Infantry Cohorts

magic_items.name: Dragonblade
magic_items.description: Dragonblade is inscribed with a Rune of Fury and a Grudge Rune, as described in Forces of Fantasy.

magic_items.name: Burning Blade*
magic_items.description:
Once unsheathed, the cutting edge of the Burning Blade writhes endlessly with a living flame.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Flaming AttacksMagical Attacks*Extremely Common
*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Biting Blade
magic_items.description:
The serrated length of the Biting Blade cuts through armour of plate and mail with terrible ease.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-2Armour Bane (1)Magical Attacks

magic_items.name: Sword of Striking*
magic_items.description:
Engraved with runes of accuracy and swiftness, the Sword of Striking is an elegant weapon of great precision.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Magical AttacksNotes: During the Combat phase, the wielder of the Sword of Striking has a +1 modifier to their rolls To Hit.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Berserker Blade
magic_items.description:
Quenched in the blood of untold fallen enemies, the Berserker Blade eternally hungers for battle.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-Extra Attacks (+1)ImpetuousMagical AttacksNotes: The wielder of the Berserker Blade is Impetuous.
Notes: The wielder of the Berserker Blade is Impetuous.

magic_items.name: Sword of Might*
magic_items.description:
Bound around with powerful runes that increase its power and enhance the strength of its bearer.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Magical Attacks*Extremely Common
*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Sword of Swiftness
magic_items.description:
Crafted from magically attuned materials and ensorcelled to strike as if with a life of its own.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Magical AttacksStrike First

magic_items.name: Giant Blade
magic_items.description:
A broad and heavy blade wrought with powerful enchantments that only increase the weight of its blows, the Giant Blade is an unsubtle weapon.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-Armour Bane (2)Magical AttacksMultiple Wounds (2)

magic_items.name: Hell-forged Axe
magic_items.description:
In the midst of battle, smoke billows and flames burst from the glowing blade of this fell weapon.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Armour Bane (3)Flaming AttacksMagical Attacks

magic_items.name: Sword of Sorrow
magic_items.description: Though it possesses no special properties when wielded in combat, it is said that a gesture with the Sword of Sorrow can pierce an enemy's heart from afar.
magic_items.restrictions: Sword of Sorrow(Magic Weapon)30 pointsThough it possesses no special properties when wielded in combat, it is said that a gesture with the Sword of Sorrow can pierce an enemy's heart from afar.RangeSt

magic_items.name: Spelleater Axe
magic_items.description:
Forged of rune-encrusted brass, the Spelleater Axe is anathema to the Winds of Magic.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-1Magical AttacksMagic Resistance (-2)

magic_items.name: Headsman's Axe
magic_items.description:
A wide bladed axe, steeped in the blood of its countless helpless victims.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Killing BlowMagical AttacksRequires Two Hands

magic_items.name: Dragon Slaying Sword
magic_items.description:
A mighty blade, wielded by many great heroes in their endless quests to slay foul monsters.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Magical AttacksMonster Slayer

magic_items.name: Meteor Hammer
magic_items.description:
No matter who wields this mighty hammer, their blows will land with the force of a meteor plummeting from the heavens.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-3Magical AttacksRequires Two HandsNotes: When making a roll To Wound with this weapon, a roll of a 3+ is always a success, regardless of the target's Toughness.

magic_items.name: Duellist's Blades
magic_items.description:
Fine weapons, forged by the greatest of swordsmiths and imbued with potent runes of quickening.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-1Extra Attacks (+2)Magical AttacksRequires Two Hands

magic_items.name: Sword of Battle
magic_items.description:
A simple weapon, yet finely crafted, blessed by mighty mages and revered priests.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Armour Bane (1)Extra Attacks (+1)Magical Attacks

magic_items.name: Cackling Blade
magic_items.description:
Those who carry the Cackling Blade relinquish all self control until the battle has long ended - often to their peril.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Extra Attacks (+D6)Magical AttacksNotes: If a natural 6 is rolled for the Extra Attacks (+D6) special rule, the wielder of the Cackling Blade immediately loses a single Wound.

magic_items.name: Ogre Blade
magic_items.description:
Magic weapons grant the wielder extra abilities, new special rules or an improved profile, the better to slay enemies. Unless otherwise stated, the following rules apply to all magic weapons:
A magic weapon cannot be used alongside an additional hand weapon, magical or mundane, to gain an extra attack.
A character that wields a magic weapon cannot set it aside to use a mundane equivalent. For example, a character cannot set aside a magic combat weapon to use a mundane great weapon instead.
magic_items.restrictions: A magic weapon cannot be used alongside an additional hand weapon, magical or mundane, to gain an extra attack.A character that wields a magic weapon cannot set it aside to use a mundane equivalent. F

magic_items.name: Charmed Shield*
magic_items.description: A simple shield hung with charms and fetishes to bring the bearer good fortune. In the heat of battle, such ornamentation rarely survives intact for long.

magic_items.name: Enchanted Shield*
magic_items.description:
A finely crafted and sturdy shield that protects its bearer from harm time and time again.
The Enchanted Shield is a shield. In addition, its bearer has a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a non-magical enemy attack.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Armour of Meteoric Iron
magic_items.description:
This heavy armour was forged of ore mined from a meteor that fell from the heavens.
The Armour of Meteoric Iron gives its wearer an armour value of 5+, which cannot be improved in any way. However, nor can this armour value be reduced in any way either.
magic_items.restrictions: Armour of Meteoric Iron(Magic Armour)20 pointsThis heavy armour was forged of ore mined from a meteor that fell from the heavens.The Armour of Meteoric Iron gives its wearer an armour value of 5+, whi

magic_items.name: Trailblazer's Hauberk
magic_items.description:
The local flora seems to shift and bend to allow the wearer to pass unimpeded through even the thickest briar.
Models whose troop type is infantry only. The Trailblazer's Hauberk is a suit of heavy armour. In addition, its wearer gains the Move Through Cover and Scouts special rules.

magic_items.name: Levitating Shield
magic_items.description:
Born aloft by a cunning enchantment, this shield seems to place itself between its owner and the weapons of the enemy.
Models whose troop type is infantry only. The Levitating Shield is a shield. In addition, the bearer may use it alongside a weapon with the Requires Two Hands special rule during combat.
magic_items.restrictions: Levitating Shield(Magic Armour)25 pointsBorn aloft by a cunning enchantment, this shield seems to place itself between its owner and the weapons of the enemy.Models whose troop type is infantry only.

magic_items.name: Spellshield*
magic_items.description:
The Winds of Magic lose their power when in the presence of the brass-bound Spellshield.
The Spellshield is a shield. In addition, its bearer has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a Magic Missile, a Magical Vortex, or an Assailment spell.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Shield of the Warrior True
magic_items.description:
A heavy shield of oak and iron that turns aside all but the most deadly of missiles.
The Shield of the Warrior True is a shield. In addition, its bearer has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered during the Shooting phase.

magic_items.name: Glittering Scales
magic_items.description:
Each and every scale of this armoured surcoat is crafted from shards of precious metal.
The Glittering Scales is a suit of light armour. In addition, once per turn, you may make your opponent re-roll a single roll To Hit made against the wearer.

magic_items.name: Armour of Silvered Steel
magic_items.description:
Polished to a mirror shine, the Armour of Silvered Steel encases its wearer from head to toe.
The Armour of Silvered Steel is a suit of armour that gives its wearer an armour value of 3+ which cannot be improved in any way.

magic_items.name: Bedazzling Helm
magic_items.description: A gleaming helm that confuses and confounds enemies, causing their blows to fall wide of their mark.
magic_items.restrictions: Bedazzling Helm(Magic Armour)60 pointsA gleaming helm that confuses and confounds enemies, causing their blows to fall wide of their mark.Models whose troop type is infantry or cavalry only. May be wo

magic_items.name: Armour of Destiny
magic_items.description: Only a model that is equipped with (or that has the option to be equipped with) armour, a shield or barding may purchase a magical equivalent. A model can only wear a single suit of armour and can only carry one shield, and a mount can only wear a single suit of barding. Therefore, magic armour replaces any mundane equivalent the model is, or may be, equipped with.

magic_items.name: Luckstone*
magic_items.description:
Can luck be trapped within a dull stone pendant? Possibly.
Single use. The bearer of a Luckstone can re-roll a single failed Armour Save roll.*Extremely Common
Single use. The bearer of a Luckstone can re-roll a single failed Armour Save roll.
*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Obsidian Lodestone*
magic_items.description:
An air of chill hangs about obsidian, stilling the Winds of Magic in its presence.
A model may purchase up to three Obsidian Lodestones. A model that bears a single Obsidian Lodestone has Magic Resistance (-1), a model that bears two has Magic Resistance (-2), and a model that bears three has Magic Resistance (-3).*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Paymaster's Coin*
magic_items.description:
This heavy golden coin may not be enchanted, but its weight in its owner's pocket certainly inspires heroism.
Single use. The bearer of a Paymaster's Coin can re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made during the Combat phase.*Extremely Common
Single use. The bearer of a Paymaster's Coin can re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made during the Combat phase.
*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Ironhide Talisman
magic_items.description:
It is said that the one who bears this powerful heirloom can endure even the most telling blows.
Models whose troop type is infantry only. Any enemy model that makes a successful roll To Hit against the bearer of the Ironhide Talisman during the Shooting phase or Combat phase must re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 6.Note that this magic item does not work against attacks that hit automatically.

magic_items.name: Talisman of Protection
magic_items.description:
A powerful magical aura surrounds this humble talisman, protecting its wearer from harm.
The Talisman of Protection gives its bearer a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

magic_items.name: Dawnstone
magic_items.description:
Talismans are tokens of magical protection, worn by warriors to protect them from harm both physical and magical.
The magic trapped within this semi-precious stone binds together shattered armour plates.
The bearer of the Dawnstone may re-roll any Armour Save roll of a natural 1.

magic_items.name: Icon of Fortitude
magic_items.description:
Carefully woven with protective enchantments, this exquisite brooch is said to shield its bearer from the deadliest blows.
Models whose troop type is infantry or cavalry only. The bearer is immune to the Multiple Wounds (X) special rule. If the bearer is wounded by an attack with this special rule, they suffer a single wound.

magic_items.name: Potion of Foolhardiness*
magic_items.description:
A magical brew that fills the drinker with courage… it may actually just be strong liquor.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Foolhardiness can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model gains the Immune To Psychology special rule.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Potion of Speed*
magic_items.description:
A powerful potion with a terrible aftertaste that sharpens the wits and speeds the reactions.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Speed can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Initiative characteristic (to a maximum of 10).*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Becalming Orb
magic_items.description:
This pulsing crystal orb can syphon the Winds of Magic away from its bearer's enemies.
Single Use. The bearer of the Becalming Orb may cast the following Bound spell, with a Power level of 1:TypeHexCasting Value8+RangeSelfUntil your next Start of Turn sub-phase, enemy Wizards that are within 15" of the caster when attempting to cast a spell must increase that spell's casting value by 2.

magic_items.name: Potion of Toughness*
magic_items.description:
Distilled by magical means, this potion is sure to make the drinker all but invulnerable.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Toughness can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Toughness characteristic (to a maximum of 10).*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Potion of Strength*
magic_items.description:
A concoction of strength-giving vegetables and roots, mixed together with a touch of magic.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Strength can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Strength characteristic (to a maximum of 10).*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Potion of Fervour
magic_items.description:
This noxious brew allows those who risk drinking it a moment of godliness upon the battlefield.
Single Use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of the Potion of Fervour can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Attacks characteristic (to a maximum of 10).Note that a model can only consume a single potion (of any type) per Command phase.

magic_items.name: Healing Potion*
magic_items.description:
Purveyors of magical potions make many bold claims. Sometimes they are true, sometimes not.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Healing Potion can consume it. The model immediately recovers D3 lost Wounds.*Extremely Common
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Healing Potion can consume it. The model immediately recovers D3 lost Wounds.

magic_items.name: Ruby Ring of Ruin
magic_items.description:
Uttering a simple command, the wearer of the ring can hurl a flaming ball of ruin at their foes.
The wielder of the Ruby Ring of Ruin can cast the Fireball spell from the Lore of Battle Magic as a Bound spell, with a Power Level of 1.

magic_items.name: Arch-Lightning Rod
magic_items.description: Crackling lightning fills the heavens with peril, grounding the creatures of the sky.
magic_items.restrictions: Arch-Lightning Rod(Enchanted Item)40 pointsCrackling lightning fills the heavens with peril, grounding the creatures of the sky.Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if they are not

magic_items.name: Flying Carpet
magic_items.description:
Woven from the finest silks in a far-off land, this splendid rug does more than warm the feet!
Models whose troop type is regular infantry or heavy infantry only. The bearer of the Flying Carpet gains the Fly (8) and Swiftstride special rules. However, the bearer cannot join a unit.
magic_items.restrictions: Flying Carpet(Enchanted Item)40 pointsWoven from the finest silks in a far-off land, this splendid rug does more than warm the feet!Models whose troop type is regular infantry or heavy infantry only.

magic_items.name: Wizarding Hat
magic_items.description:
The enchanted items category includes all manner of wonderful artefacts too unique or specific to be included in another category. Enchanted items are often amongst the most prized magical items a character might possess.
Note that a model can only consume a single potion (of any type) per Command phase.
This splendid hat, once the property of a long dead Wizard, is still haunted by their ghost.

magic_items.name: Earthing Rod
magic_items.description:
Inert metal runs the length of the Wizard's staff, drawing magical overloads safely into the earth.
Single use. Should they miscast a spell, a Wizard can use the Earthing Rod to re-roll the result rolled on the Miscast table.

magic_items.name: Arcane Familiar
magic_items.description: Many Wizards are accompanied by familiars gifted with arcane knowledge. These creatures constantly whisper secrets to their master.

magic_items.name: Dispel Scroll*
magic_items.description:
The Wizard reads aloud a charm of unbinding from an enchanted scroll.
Single use. A Wizard may use this scroll when attempting a Wizardly dispel. If they do so, roll an extra D6 when making the Dispel roll and discard the lowest result. However, if a double 1 is rolled on any two of the dice rolled, the Wizard is outclassed in the art.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Power Scroll*
magic_items.description:
Unfurling a scroll bearing runes of power can greatly increase the spell's power.
Single use. A Wizard may use this scroll when attempting to cast a spell. If they do so, roll an extra D6 when making the Casting roll and discard the lowest result. However, if a double 1 is rolled on any two of the dice rolled, the spell is miscast.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Lore Familiar
magic_items.description:
By creating a magical homunculus, a Wizard is able to better recall forgotten details of their studies.
The owner of a Lore Familiar does not randomly generate their spells. Instead, they may choose which spells they know from their chosen lore (including that lore's signature spell).

magic_items.name: Scroll of Disruption
magic_items.description:
When spoken aloud, the words upon this ancient scroll cause the Winds of Magic to bluster and the spells of the enemy to unravel.
Once per turn, the bearer of the Scroll of Disruption may re-roll a Dispel roll.*Note all dice are re-rolled, including any bonus or discard dice granted by special rules or magic items.

magic_items.name: Tome of Spellcraft
magic_items.description:
Reading aloud from a dusty tome of great age, the Wizard enunciates their spells with unrivalled clarity and precision.
Once per turn, the bearer of the Tome of Spellcraft may re-roll a Casting roll.*Note all dice are re-rolled, including any bonus or discard dice granted by special rules or magic items.

magic_items.name: Staff of Quietude
magic_items.description: With a gesture, the bearer of the Staff of Quietude calms the Winds of Magic, causing spells and conjurations to dissipate into the other.

magic_items.name: Wand of Jet
magic_items.description: Carved from brittle jet, this wand refines a Wizard's power, but is easily broken.
magic_items.restrictions: Wand of Jet(Arcane Item)45 pointsCarved from brittle jet, this wand refines a Wizard's power, but is easily broken.The bearer of the Wand of Jet may apply a +1 modifier to any of their Casting or Disp

magic_items.name: Scroll of Transmogrification*
magic_items.description: For a Wizard, there are few things better than the rush of joy felt upon turning a rival into a frog!
magic_items.restrictions: Scroll of Transmogrification*(Arcane Item)50 pointsFor a Wizard, there are few things better than the rush of joy felt upon turning a rival into a frog!Single use. The bearer may use this scroll inste

magic_items.name: Feedback Scroll
magic_items.description:
Arcane items are items that enhance a Wizard's magical powers in some fashion. Only Wizards can purchase Arcane Items.
When read aloud, this charm causes ætheric energy to flood back towards the enemy Wizard.
magic_items.restrictions: Arcane items are items that enhance a Wizard's magical powers in some fashion. Only Wizards can purchase Arcane Items.

magic_items.name: Monster Hunter's Tapestry
magic_items.description:
Those who have earned the right to fight under this standard have battled some of the Old World's most dangerous beasts and lived to tell the tale.
Enemy units may not make Stomp Attacks against a unit carrying the Monster Hunter's Tapestry.

magic_items.name: The Blazing Banner
magic_items.description:
An eternal flame flickers about the weapons of those that march beneath the Blazing Banner.
A unit carrying the Blazing Banner gains the Flaming Attacks special rule.

magic_items.name: War Banner
magic_items.description:
A proud banner of great age. Those that march beneath the War Banner fight with grim resolve.
When calculating its combat result, a unit carrying the War Banner may claim an additional bonus of +1 combat result point.

magic_items.name: Banner of Swirling Wind
magic_items.description:
Gale-force winds surge around this standard so fiercely that arrows and shot are blown forcefully away from their target.
Any enemy model that shoots at a unit carrying the Banner of Swirling Wind suffers an additional -1 To Hit modifier.

magic_items.name: Rampaging Banner
magic_items.description:
As a matter of honour, those that fight beneath the Rampaging Banner are always first into the fray.
When a unit carrying the Rampaging Banner declares a charge, it may re-roll its Charge roll.

magic_items.name: Razor Standard
magic_items.description:
Hung with hundreds of wickedly sharp blades, an air of sharpness radiates outwards from the Razor Standard.
A unit carrying the Razor Standard gains the Armour Bane (2) special rule.

magic_items.name: Banner of Iron Resolve
magic_items.description:
Some units have the option to purchase magic standards. To do so, the unit must include a standard bearer. The only character that can carry a magic standard is your army's Battle Standard Bearer.
No regiment that stands beneath the Banner of Iron Resolve has ever fled from the foe.
A unit carrying the Banner of Iron Resolve gains the Stubborn special rule.
magic_items.restrictions: Some units have the option to purchase magic standards. To do so, the unit must include a standard bearer. The only character that can carry a magic standard is your army's Battle Standard Bearer.

magic_items.name: Banner of the Steadfast
magic_items.description: Woven into the bright fabric of this banner is a scene of unimaginable gallantry, a scene that serves to inspire those who fight in its shadow to hold their ground no matter the odds.
magic_items.restrictions: Banner of the Steadfast(Magic Standard)50 pointsWoven into the bright fabric of this banner is a scene of unimaginable gallantry, a scene that serves to inspire those who fight in its shadow to hold t

magic_items.name: Totem of Wrath
magic_items.description:
Cruel runes etched into the surface of this icon tremble with power, causing those around it to succumb to an animalistic ferocity.
During a turn in which it charged, models within a unit carrying the Totem of Wrath improve the Armour Piercing characteristic of their weapons by 1, and may re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 1.
magic_items.restrictions: Totem of Wrath(Magic Standard)50 pointsCruel runes etched into the surface of this icon tremble with power, causing those around it to succumb to an animalistic ferocity.During a turn in which it char

magic_items.name: Curse-Weaver Wand
magic_items.description: The bearer of the Curse-Weaver Wand gains a +1 modifier to their Casting roll when attempting to cast a Hex spell.

magic_items.name: Death Mask of Kharnutt
magic_items.description: The wearer of the Death Mask of Kharnutt gains the Terror special rule.

magic_items.name: Mirage Banner
magic_items.description: Any enemy model that targets a unit carrying the Mirage Banner during the Shooting phase suffers an additional -1 To Hit modifier.

magic_items.name: Scarab Brooch
magic_items.description: When using the From Beneath the Sands special rule, a summoned unit can be placed on the battlefield anywhere completely within 18" of a Liche Priest wearing the Scarab Brooch, rather than the usual 12".

magic_items.name: Serpent Staff
magic_items.description: Combat, S+2, AP -2; Magical Attacks, Poisoned Attacks, Requires Two Hands. Liche Priests only.

magic_items.name: Tablets of Tahoth*
magic_items.description: Extremely Common. A Wizard may purchase up to three Tablets of Tahoth, each of which is single use. Before making a Casting roll, a Wizard may choose to use any number of their Tablets. For each Tablet used, you may apply a +1 modifier to the result of the Casting roll. Note that this is a modifier to the result of a roll — it does not negate a roll of a natural double 1.

magic_items.name: Collar of Shapesh
magic_items.description: Single use. When the wearer of the Collar of Shapesh loses their last Wound, roll a D6. On a roll of 4+, the Wound is not lost. Instead, a single friendly model within the wearer's Command range is removed from play as a casualty.

magic_items.name: Hieratic Jar*
magic_items.description: Extremely Common. Single use. During the Command sub-phase, if not engaged in combat, the bearer of a Hieratic Jar can release the power held within it. The bearer may attempt to resurrect the fallen by using the Arise! special rule twice during this Command sub-phase (rather than the usual once).

magic_items.name: Relic of the Desert Sun
magic_items.description: The bearer of the Relic of the Desert Sun is not subject to the Dry as Dust or Flammable special rules.

magic_items.name: Shield of Ptra
magic_items.description: Shield. In addition, any enemy model that directs their attacks against the bearer during the Combat phase suffers a -1 modifier to their Weapon Skill characteristic.

magic_items.name: Amulet of the Serpent
magic_items.description: The bearer of the Amulet of the Serpent and any unit they have joined gains the Poisoned Attacks special rule.

magic_items.name: Banner of the Desert Winds
magic_items.description: Models whose troop type is 'infantry' only. A unit carrying the Banner of the Desert Winds gains the Vanguard and Reserve Move special rules.

magic_items.name: Crown of Kings*
magic_items.description: Extremely Common. Monarch of Nehekhara only. During the Command sub-phase, if not engaged in combat, the wearer may attempt to resurrect the fallen (Arise!) by making a Leadership test. If passed, a single friendly unit of Skeleton Warriors, Skeleton Archers, Skeleton Horsemen or Skeleton Horse Archers that is within this model's Command range recovers D3+1 Wounds.

magic_items.name: Enkhil's Kanopi
magic_items.description: Single use. During the Command sub-phase, if not engaged in combat, the bearer may attempt to unleash its contents by making a Leadership test (using their own, unmodified Leadership). If passed, all 'Remains in Play' spells currently in play are dispelled, including spells cast by friendly Wizards.

magic_items.name: Staff of Aeons
magic_items.description: Combat, S+2, AP -1; Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands. May only be taken by a Liche Priest in a Mortuary Cult Army of Infamy. Any model that is hit by one or more close combat attacks made using this weapon suffers a -1 modifier to its armour value for the remainder of the game.

magic_items.name: Flail of Skulls
magic_items.description: Combat, S+3, AP -1; Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2), Requires Two Hands. The Flail of Skulls' Strength modifier applies only during the first round of combat.

magic_items.name: Icon of Rulership*
magic_items.description: Extremely Common. Models whose troop type is 'chariot' only. This model doubles its Unit Strength, from 3 to 6. In addition, any Impact Hits caused by this model have an Armour Piercing characteristic of -2 and the Magical Attacks special rule.

magic_items.name: Phakth's Blades of Justice
magic_items.description: Combat, S, AP -1; Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands. Models whose troop type is 'infantry' only. Phakth's Blades of Justice grant the wielder +1 Attack for each enemy unit the wielder is engaged with.

magic_items.name: Tapestry of Conquered Lands
magic_items.description: Any enemy standard captured by a unit carrying the Tapestry of Conquered Lands is worth 100 Victory Points as a trophy of war.

magic_items.name: Warding Splint
magic_items.description: A suit of heavy armour which may be purchased and worn by a Liche Priest, even if they do not have the option to be equipped with armour, without penalty. In addition, its wearer has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

magic_items.name: Icon of Rakaph
magic_items.description: Unless making a charge move, a unit carrying the Icon of Rakaph may perform a single free reform at any point during its movement.

magic_items.name: Orb of Ptra
magic_items.description: Any enemy model that targets this character or any unit they have joined during the Shooting phase suffers an additional -1 To Hit modifier.

magic_items.name: Phazerakt's Kanopi
magic_items.description: Single use. During the Command sub-phase, if not engaged in combat, the bearer of Phazerakt's Kanopi may attempt to unleash its contents by making a Leadership test (using their own, unmodified Leadership). If passed, place a unit of 2D6+3 Summoned Skeleton Warriors on the battlefield anywhere completely within 12" of this model, but not within 1" of any enemy models. Summoned Skeleton Warriors have: M4, WS2, BS2, S3, T3, W1, I2, A1, Ld5; Troop Type Regular infantry; Base Size 25x25mm; Unit Size 2D6+3; Equipment: Hand weapons and shields; Special Rules: Horde, Nehekharan Undead, Regeneration (6+), Skirmishers. Summoned Skeleton Warriors are not worth any Victory Points.

magic_items.name: Royal Mantle
magic_items.description: May only be taken by models in a Nehekharan Royal Host Army of Infamy. May be worn with other armour. The wearer of the Royal Mantle improves their armour value by 1 (to a maximum of 2+). In addition, the wearer's My Will Be Done special rule affects all friendly units with the Nehekharan Undead special rule within 6" of them, not just a unit they have joined.

magic_items.name: Blade of Antarhak
magic_items.description: Combat, S+1, AP -1; Magical Attacks. May only be taken by a model in a Nehekharan Royal Host Army of Infamy. For each Wound an enemy unit loses as a result of an attack made with the Blade of Antarhak, its wielder immediately recovers a single lost Wound.

magic_items.name: Sigil of Centuries
magic_items.description: All enemy units within 6" of the model carrying the Sigil of Centuries suffer a -1 modifier to their Initiative characteristic (to a minimum of 1).

magic_items.name: Armour of the Ages
magic_items.description: Suit of light armour. In addition, enemy models must re-roll successful rolls To Wound made against the wearer.

magic_items.name: Cloak of the Dunes
magic_items.description: Models whose troop type is 'infantry' only. The wearer gains the Fly (9) special rule. In addition, any enemy unit the wearer moves over during the Remaining Moves sub-phase suffers D6 Strength 2 hits, each with an AP of -1.

magic_items.name: Crook & Flail of Radiance
magic_items.description: Combat, S, AP -1; Extra Attacks (+D3), Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands, Strike First. Monarchs of Nehekhara only.

magic_items.name: Icon of the Sacred Eye
magic_items.description: A unit carrying the Icon of the Sacred Eye has a +1 modifier to its Weapon Skill characteristic (to a maximum of 10).

magic_items.name: Royal Standard of Settra
magic_items.description: May only be taken in a muster list that includes Settra the Imperishable and/or Nekaph, Emissary of Settra. A unit carrying the Royal Standard of Settra gains the Hatred (enemy characters) and Terror special rules.

magic_items.name: Staff of Awakening
magic_items.description: High Priest only. When the wielder of the Staff of Awakening uses the Arise! special rule on a friendly unit whose troop type is 'infantry' or 'cavalry', that unit recovers an additional D3 Wounds.

magic_items.name: The Conqueror's Blade
magic_items.description: Combat, S+2, AP -2; Killing Blow, Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands, Strike Last. Whilst engaged in a challenge, the bearer strikes a Killing Blow if they roll a natural 5 or 6 when making a roll To Wound, rather than the usual 6. In addition, should the wielder slay the enemy General in a challenge, you win a bonus of 100 Victory Points at the end of the game.

magic_items.name: Destroyer of Eternities
magic_items.description: Combat, S+2, AP -2; Killing Blow, Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands, Strike Last. Rather than attacking normally, the wielder may make a special 'Scything' attack. If they do, the enemy unit they are directing their attacks against suffers D6 automatic hits, each resolved using the Destroyer of Eternities' profile.

magic_items.name: Standard of the Cursing Word
magic_items.description: Battle Standard Bearer only. At the end of any phase in which one or more models in a unit joined by the bearer lost its last Wound to an enemy attack, the unit that made the attack must make a Leadership test. If failed, the enemy unit suffers D3 Strength 2 hits, each with an AP of -, for each model that lost its last Wound.

lore_of_nehekhara.name: Djaf's Incantation of Cursed Blades
lore_of_nehekhara.effect: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, any friendly unit that has the Nehekharan Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1.

lore_of_nehekhara.name: Khsar's Incantation of the Desert Wind
lore_of_nehekhara.effect: If cast with a casting result of 6 or more, a single friendly unit that has the Nehekharan Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Reserve Move special rule until the end of this turn. If cast with a casting result of 10 or more, every friendly unit that has the Nehekharan Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Reserve Move special rule until the end of this turn.

lore_of_nehekhara.name: Usekhp's Incantation of Desiccation
lore_of_nehekhara.effect: Until the end of this turn, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to its Strength and Toughness characteristics (to a minimum of 1). If this spell is cast, the effects of any other Hex previously cast on the target unit immediately expire.
